Vacation (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $58,884,188Details
International Box Office $46,000,000
Worldwide Box Office $104,884,188
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,860,576
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,224,157
Total $11,084,733
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Following in his father’s footsteps and hoping for some much-needed family bonding, a grown-up Rusty Griswold surprises his wife and their two sons with a cross country trip back to America's ”favorite family fun park,” Walley World.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$14,681,108 (24.9% of total gross)
Legs:4.01 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:56.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$31,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,411 opening theaters/3,430 max. theaters, 4.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $83,541,509

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 29th, 2015 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: July 30th, 2015 (Wide) (Argentina)
July 31st, 2015 (Wide) (Venezuela)
August 5th, 2015 (Wide) (Norway)
August 6th, 2015 (Wide) (Hong Kong)
August 6th, 2015 (Wide) (Singapore)
Video Release: October 13th, 2015 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for crude and sexual content and language throughout, and brief graphic nudity.
(Rating bulletin 2380, 7/1/2015)
Running Time: 98 minutes
Franchise: National Lampoon's Vacation
Cast, crew, or production detail: Actors Making Their Directorial Debut, Legacy Sequel, Sequel With Lead Character Recast
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, Road Trip
Social setting: Vacation
Subgenre: Family Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Benderspink Productions, Big Kid Pictures, New Line Cinema, RatPac Entertainment, Dune Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Following in his father’s footsteps and hoping for some much-needed family bonding, a grown-up Rusty Griswold surprises his wife and their two sons with a cross country trip back to America's ”favorite family fun park,” Walley World.
